overview.tagline: A small, convenient township at the crossroads of Bergen County
overview.description: Rochelle Park is one of the smallest townships in Bergen County, spanning less than one square mile, but punches above its weight in convenience and accessibility. Located at the intersection of Route 17 and Route 4, the township offers unparalleled access to Bergen County's major shopping destinations, highways, and public transit. Despite its compact size, Rochelle Park maintains a strong residential character with well-kept homes, a dedicated community, and low-key suburban living just minutes from Hackensack and Paramus.
overview.schoolDistrict: Rochelle Park Public Schools (K-6) / Hackensack Public Schools (7-12)
highlights: Strategic location at Routes 4 and 17 crossroads
Minutes from Garden State Plaza and Bergen Town Center shopping
Compact, affordable community with low-key suburban feel
Easy access to NJ Transit bus routes to NYC
Active community events and engaged local government

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Bergen County Tax Assessor's office. The township features predominantly single-family homes and some multi-family residences on small to mid-sized lots.
realEstate.recentTrends: Rochelle Park offers some of the most affordable housing in central Bergen County, attracting first-time buyers and those looking for value. Prices have risen moderately in recent years as demand for Bergen County housing remains strong. The compact lot sizes and older housing stock keep prices accessible.
neighborhoodNotes: Rochelle Avenue area — Central residential area near township facilities
Route 17 corridor — Commercial frontage with residential streets behind
Northern section — Quieter residential streets near the Paramus border
Southern section — Close to Route 4 and shopping centers

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup is provided twice weekly by the Township of Rochelle Park.
details: Pickup occurs twice per week on designated days
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use approved containers with secure lids
Bulk item pickup available by appointment
Holiday schedule adjustments posted on township website

recycling.title: Recycling
details: Commingled recyclables collected weekly
Paper and cardboard collected on recycling day
Recycling bins available from the Township
Yard waste collected seasonally
Electronics and hazardous waste drop-off events held periodically

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is provided by Veolia Water and sewer service is maintained by the Township.
details: Water supplied by Veolia Water (formerly Suez/United Water)
Sewer service maintained by the Township DPW
Water billing handled by Veolia quarterly
Sewer charges included on property tax bills
Emergency water repairs available 24/7

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles construction permits, zoning, and code enforcement.
details: Building permits required for construction and renovations
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board
Permit applications available at Township Hall
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department
Certificate of Occupancy required for new construction

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: Parking is regulated on township streets and in commercial areas.
details: Street parking subject to posted regulations
Overnight parking restrictions on township streets
Snow emergency parking bans enforced
No metered parking in residential areas
Commercial area parking regulated by posted signs

additionalServices.title: Rochelle Park Public Library
additionalServices.description: Public library offering books, digital resources, and community programs.
details: Located at 151 Rochelle Avenue
Children's programs, adult events, and book clubs
Free Wi-Fi and computer access
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)

additionalServices.title: Recreation Department
additionalServices.description: Recreational programs for residents of all ages.
details: Youth sports leagues and summer programs
Senior citizen programs and activities
Community events throughout the year
Township park facilities available for recreation

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Rochelle Park enforces noise regulations to protect residential quality of life.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM only
No construction noise on Sundays or federal holidays
Loud music and amplified sound restricted after 10:00 PM
Leaf blowers and power equipment restricted to daytime hours

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties in good condition.
keyRules: Lawns must be maintained and grass kept at a reasonable height
Property owners responsible for sidewalk snow removal within 24 hours
Fences must comply with township height regulations
Abandoned or inoperable vehicles must be removed
Properties must be kept free of rubbish and debris

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Dog licensing and leash laws are enforced throughout the township.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed annually with rabies vaccination proof
Dogs must be leashed on all public property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately
Excessive barking may result in violation notices
No exotic or wild animals permitted as pets

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Street parking is regulated throughout the township.
keyRules: No overnight parking on township streets between 2:00 AM and 6:00 AM
Snow emergency parking bans enforced
Commercial vehicles prohibited from residential street parking overnight
Vehicles may not block sidewalks or driveways
Abandoned vehicles subject to towing

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must be registered and comply with housing codes.
keyRules: Rental properties must be registered with the Township
Certificate of Habitability required before occupancy
Landlords must maintain properties to housing code standards
Inspections conducted at change of tenancy
Occupancy limits enforced per unit size

other.title: Garage Sales
other.summary: Garage and yard sales are permitted with restrictions.
keyRules: Permit required for garage/yard sales
Limited to specified number of sales per year
Signs must be removed after the sale
Sales restricted to daylight hours

schools.publicDistrict: Rochelle Park Public Schools (K-6) / Hackensack Public Schools (7-12)

publicSchools.name: Midland School
publicSchools.notes: The township's sole elementary school serving all K-6 students; students attend Hackensack schools for grades 7-12

publicSchools.name: Hackensack Middle School
publicSchools.notes: Regional middle school in Hackensack serving Rochelle Park students

publicSchools.name: Hackensack High School
publicSchools.notes: Regional high school serving Rochelle Park students along with Hackensack and South Hackensack

privateSchools.name: St. Joseph School (East Rutherford)
privateSchools.notes: Catholic school serving the surrounding area, including Rochelle Park families

whyMoveHere: Affordable housing in a central Bergen County location
Unmatched access to major shopping centers (Garden State Plaza, Bergen Town Center)
Easy highway access via Routes 4, 17, and the Garden State Parkway
Small, tight-knit community with engaged local government
Close to NJ Transit bus routes for NYC commuting

commuteToNYC.duration: 35-55 minutes to Midtown Manhattan by bus
commuteToNYC.notes: NJ Transit bus routes provide service from nearby stops on Route 17 and Route 4 to the Port Authority Bus Terminal. No train station within the township, but the Hackensack bus terminal is nearby. Highway access via Routes 4, 17, and 80 provides driving options.

costOfLiving.summary: Rochelle Park is one of the more affordable communities in central Bergen County, offering good value for its location.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Approximately 10% above the New Jersey state average

pros: Very affordable by Bergen County standards
Central location with excellent highway access
Minutes from major shopping and dining destinations
Small community with a neighborhood feel
Multiple bus routes for NYC commuting
cons: No train station within the township
Highway noise from Routes 4 and 17 affects some areas
Students attend Hackensack schools for grades 7-12
Very small township with limited parks and open space
Higher property tax rate compared to some neighbors

restaurants.name: Park Avenue Diner
restaurants.description: Classic New Jersey diner serving breakfast, lunch, and dinner with a comprehensive American menu.

restaurants.name: Bensi Ristorante
restaurants.description: Italian restaurant offering traditional pasta dishes, brick-oven pizza, and classic Italian-American fare.

restaurants.name: Green Fusion
restaurants.description: Asian fusion restaurant serving sushi, Thai curry, and creative fusion dishes in a modern setting.

restaurants.name: El Pollo Camero
restaurants.description: Latin American restaurant specializing in rotisserie chicken, plantains, and traditional Latin favorites.

parks.name: Rochelle Park Township Park
parks.description: Township park with playground equipment, sports courts, and green space for community use.

parks.name: Veterans Memorial
parks.description: Memorial site honoring local veterans with installations and a small gathering area.

parks.name: Saddle River County Park (nearby)
parks.description: Bergen County park stretching along the Saddle River with walking trails, sports fields, and picnic areas, accessible from Rochelle Park.

events.name: Rochelle Park Community Day
events.description: Annual township celebration with food, entertainment, games, and family activities.

events.name: National Night Out
events.description: Annual event promoting police-community partnerships with activities, demonstrations, and neighborhood gatherings.

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: Annual holiday celebration with tree lighting, caroling, and community festivities.

attractions.name: Garden State Plaza (adjacent)
attractions.description: One of the largest shopping malls in New Jersey, located in adjacent Paramus with over 300 stores, restaurants, and entertainment.

attractions.name: Bergen Town Center (nearby)
attractions.description: Major shopping center in Paramus featuring department stores, specialty shops, and dining options.

shoppingAreas: Route 17 corridor — Major retail and dining strip
Garden State Plaza (Paramus) — Mega-mall adjacent to Rochelle Park
Bergen Town Center (Paramus) — Large shopping center nearby
Route 4 corridor — Additional retail and commercial options

government.meetingSchedule: Township Council meets on the 2nd and 4th Tuesday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Township Hall, 151 Rochelle Avenue, Rochelle Park, NJ 07662

importantNumbers.label: Bergen County Office of Emergency Management
